seo.title: Alaska Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Alaska — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license alaska, dental assistant certification alaska, RDA alaska, DANB CDA alaska, dental assistant radiography alaska

board.name: Alaska Board of Dental Examiners
board.mailing_address:
Department of Commerce, Community, and Economic Development
Division of Corporations, Business and Professional Licensing
PO Box 110806
Juneau AK, 99811-0806

license_types.title: Dental Assistant with Restorative Functions
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Alaska.

steps.title: Understand Alaska's Dental Regulations
steps.description: Familiarize yourself with the Alaska Dental Practice Act (AS 08.36 and 12 AAC 28), which outlines the scope of practice for dentists and the duties they may legally delegate to dental assistants. No state license is required for dental assistants in Alaska.

steps.title: Seek Employment with a Licensed Alaska Dentist
steps.description: Dental assistants in Alaska operate under the direct supervision and delegation of a licensed Alaska dentist. Employment is secured directly with dental practices, not through state licensing.

steps.title: Receive On-the-Job Training
steps.description: Alaska does not mandate specific education or training requirements for dental assistants. Training for delegated procedures is typically provided on-the-job by the supervising dentist, consistent with state regulations.

steps.title: Adhere to Delegated Duties
steps.description: Ensure that any procedures performed are within the scope of duties legally delegable by the supervising dentist, as defined by the Alaska Dental Practice Act and regulations enforced by the Alaska Board of Dental Examiners.

steps.title: Consider Voluntary National Certification
steps.description: Although not state-mandated, dental assistants may pursue national certification through the Dental Assisting National Board (DANB) for professional development and to demonstrate competency. This is an optional credential.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Dental Assistant with Restorative Functions through the Alaska Board of Dental Examiners by certification.

faqs.question: Does Alaska require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Alaska requires Dental Assistant with Restorative Functions through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Alaska?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Alaska.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Alaska?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Alaska?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $60. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Dental Assistant with Restorative Functions through the Alaska Board of Dental Examiners by certification.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Alaska
hero.dek: Dental Assistants are not required to be state-licensed in Alaska. Therefore, there is no state application portal or formal licensing process through the Alaska Board of Dental Examiners.

official_links.portal_name: No State Licensing Portal for Dental Assistants
official_links.registration_steps: There is no state-level online application portal or registration process for Dental Assistants in Alaska, as they are not state-licensed by the Alaska Board of Dental Examiners.
